talk_title: CAR T‑cell consolidation therapy for older ALL patients and BAFF‑R CAR for CD19‑negative relapse

speaker.name: Steve Forman
speaker.affiliation: City of Hope, Department of Hematology and Oncology, Duarte, CA

biomarkers.name: PH positivity
biomarkers.finding: Patients with PH‑positive disease (n=10) achieved molecular remission after BAFF‑R CAR infusion

biomarkers.name: MRD (ClonoSEQ)
biomarkers.finding: Two molecular relapses detected by ClonoSEQ; both patients were retreated and proceeded to transplant

models.name: Phase 1 CD19 CAR consolidation trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Adults >55 yr, in first remission after standard chemotherapy, prior blinatumomab exposure

models.name: First‑in‑human BAFF‑R CAR trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Relapsed CD19‑negative ALL or B‑cell lymphoma, BAFF‑R expression ≥10% by IHC

clinical_data.population: Adults with relapsed ALL receiving CD19 CAR
CD19 CAR in first remission (low disease burden)
Older ALL patients treated with CD19 CAR consolidation

clinical_data.metric: Objective response (CR/PR) in BAFF‑R CAR lymphoma cohort
clinical_data.population: Patients with active B‑cell lymphoma receiving BAFF‑R CAR

MOA_summary: CD19 CAR T cells with a naive‑phenotype enrichment expand, contract, and persist for 4–5 months, trafficking to CSF and providing durable remission in older ALL patients when given in first remission; BAFF‑R CAR T cells target an essential B‑cell survival receptor, showing expansion, low neurotoxicity, and clinical activity in CD19‑negative relapsed B‑cell malignancies.
key_findings: In a cohort of 48 older ALL patients, CD19 CAR achieved 90% complete remission with no dose‑limiting toxicities and markedly reduced grade ≥2 CRS (17% vs 80% in relapse setting).
CAR T cells were detectable in CSF of every treated patient, indicating CNS trafficking and no observed CNS relapses.
Two molecular relapses were identified by ClonoSEQ; both patients were successfully retreated and proceeded to allogeneic transplant.
First‑in‑human BAFF‑R CAR demonstrated expansion comparable to CD19 CAR, grade 1 toxicities only, and ≥70% objective responses in lymphoma patients.
BAFF‑R expression persisted in ~66% of CD19‑negative ALL relapses, supporting its role as a salvage target.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: CD19 loss or epitope alteration accounted for ~30% of post‑blinatumomab relapses, necessitating BAFF‑R targeting.
Variable BAFF‑R antigen density in ALL (≈80% of cases) may limit efficacy in low‑expressors.

combination_strategies.rationale: Enhance CAR T‑cell engraftment and expansion
combination_strategies.phenotype: Improved in vivo persistence and reduced toxicity

open_questions: Will longer‑term follow‑up confirm durability of remission beyond 2 years in older ALL patients?
Can BAFF‑R CAR be combined with CD19 CAR or bispecific antibodies to prevent antigen escape?

talk_title: Engineering TCR‑engineered T cells to overcome TGF‑β–driven dysfunction in solid tumors

speaker.name: Phil Greenberg
speaker.affiliation: Fred Hutchinson Cancer Research Center, Seattle, WA

targets.dependency_context: renal cell carcinoma, ovarian, pancreatic, glioblastoma

biomarkers.name: ctDNA
biomarkers.finding: Complete clearance in 2 of 4 KRAS‑TCR patients; relapse observed at day 60–100 in one patient

biomarkers.name: CEA
biomarkers.finding: Marked decline correlating with tumor shrinkage in KRAS‑TCR treated pancreatic cancer patients

models.name: KRAS G12V TCR trial (A11‑restricted)
models.genotype_or_key_features: HLA‑A2+, KRAS G12V+, n=4 treated, dose‑escalation

models.name: Mesothelin TCR trial (pancreatic cancer)
models.genotype_or_key_features: 9 patients, stable disease in 6, no PR/CR

models.name: KPC genetically engineered mouse model
models.genotype_or_key_features: KRAS G12D; Trp53 R172H; pancreatic adenocarcinoma

models.name: PDX models of RCC, ovarian, pancreatic, glioblastoma

clinical_data.value: ≥30% reduction in target lesion in 2 of 4 KRAS‑TCR patients
high in tumor‑resident engineered T cells vs low in blood

MOA_summary: Engineered TCR T cells targeting mesothelin or KRAS G12V infiltrate pancreatic tumors but acquire a TGF‑β–driven, mitigated exhaustion program; CRISPR knockout of the TGF‑β‑responsive transcription factor IKZF3 (Aeolus) reduces both TGF‑β and exhaustion signatures but induces apoptosis, which can be rescued by synthetic fusion proteins delivering pro‑survival signals. HIT CARs replace the scFv with antibody heavy/light chains to create HLA‑independent, ultra‑sensitive receptors that eradicate low‑density CD70‑expressing tumors in PDX models, outperforming conventional CARs.
key_findings: Mesothelin‑specific TCR T cells showed tumor infiltration but displayed a dominant TGF‑β–driven exhaustion signature distinct from blood T cells.

IKZF3 (Aeolus) knockout eliminated the TGF‑β signature and mitigated exhaustion but caused activation‑induced cell death, which was rescued by engineered fusion proteins delivering IL‑2 or FasL‑derived survival signals.
HIT CD70 CARs demonstrated superior sensitivity, achieving tumor eradication in renal cell carcinoma PDXs with low CD70 expression where conventional CARs failed.
KRAS G12V‑specific TCRs engineered with CD8αβ and CD28 co‑stimulation induced durable tumor regressions and biomarker (CEA, ctDNA) declines in 3 of 4 patients.
Spatial analysis revealed that exhausted engineered T cells reside in tumor cores surrounded by TGF‑β, whereas functional T cells localize to tertiary lymphoid structures near vasculature.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: TGF‑β in the pancreatic TME drives a unique exhaustion program not seen in melanoma TILs.
CD70 expression is epigenetically silenced in kidney‑site RCC PDXs via EZH2‑mediated H3K27me3, leading to antigen loss and resistance to conventional CARs.

combination_strategies.rationale: Restore avidity of CD4‑engineered TCRs to match CD8 T cells
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced tumor killing and cytokine production in vitro

combination_strategies.rationale: Provide secondary signal to CD4 TCR‑engineered cells
combination_strategies.phenotype: Improved persistence and tumor clearance in mouse models

combination_strategies.with_agent: Synthetic fusion proteins (e.g., TGF‑β/IL‑2 chimeric receptor)
combination_strategies.rationale: Convert suppressive TGF‑β signals into proliferative IL‑2 signals
combination_strategies.phenotype: Sustained cytotoxicity and proliferation over 150 h in vitro

open_questions: Can Aeolus knockout combined with survival‑signal fusion proteins achieve durable persistence without apoptosis in patients?
What is the optimal balance of TGF‑β signaling to maintain stemness versus exhaustion in solid‑tumor TCR therapies?
Will HIT CARs maintain specificity and safety in vivo given their ultra‑high sensitivity to low antigen density?

talk_title: Novel CAR designs: 1XX, HIT, and 1A platforms for enhanced potency and sensitivity

targets.dependency_context: renal cell carcinoma, ovarian, pancreatic, glioblastoma
renal cell carcinoma, ovarian, breast, glioblastoma

clinical_data.population: Patients with refractory DLBCL receiving 25 M 1XX CAR T cells

clinical_data.metric: Tumor burden reduction (HIT CD70 CAR, RCC PDX)
clinical_data.value: Complete eradication in kidney model at 5–10 M cells
clinical_data.population: Renal cell carcinoma PDX with low CD70

MOA_summary: The 1XX CAR removes two of three CD3ζ ITAMs, delivering a calibrated activation signal that enables durable complete remissions with a 25‑million‑cell dose and reduced cytokine toxicity; HIT CARs fuse antibody heavy/light chains to a TCR scaffold, creating HLA‑independent receptors with ultra‑high antigen sensitivity that overcome low‑density CD70 expression; 1A CAR incorporates the pre‑TCR cytoplasmic domain, driving YBX1‑mediated global translation enhancement, resulting in potent, long‑lasting effector function with minimal exhaustion.
key_findings: 1XX CD19 CAR achieved a 75% complete remission rate in refractory DLBCL using only 25 M cells, with markedly lower CRS/ICANS compared to standard products.
HIT CD70 CAR eradicated renal cell carcinoma PDXs with low CD70 expression, whereas conventional CD70 CARs failed even at 10 M cell doses.
1A CD70 CAR outperformed classic 28ζ CARs in orthotopic kidney, ovarian, breast, and glioblastoma models, showing sustained tumor control and reduced exhaustion markers.
YBX1 phosphorylation uniquely sustained translation efficiency in 1A CAR T cells, linking enhanced ribosomal biogenesis to prolonged effector function.
Epigenetic silencing (EZH2‑mediated H3K27me3) of CD70 in kidney‑site tumors creates a microenvironment‑dependent antigen loss that is overcome by the heightened sensitivity of HIT CARs.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: HIT CARs’ ultra‑sensitivity may increase risk of on‑target off‑tumor effects; low CD70 expression detected in esophageal epithelium raises safety considerations.
1XX CAR’s reduced ITAM signaling mitigates cytokine release but may limit maximal cytotoxic potency in high‑antigen‑density settings.

combination_strategies.rationale: Prevent epigenetic silencing of CD70 to enhance antigen availability for HIT CARs
combination_strategies.phenotype: Increased CD70 surface density and improved tumor clearance in RCC PDX

combination_strategies.with_agent: IL‑2 cytokine support via TGF‑β/IL‑2 chimeric receptor
combination_strategies.rationale: Provide pro‑survival signaling to 1XX or 1A CAR T cells
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced persistence and reduced apoptosis in mouse models

open_questions: Will the 1XX CAR’s lower activation threshold be sufficient for high‑antigen‑density tumors without compromising efficacy?
What safety monitoring is required for HIT CARs given potential low‑level CD70 expression in normal tissues such as esophagus?
Can YBX1 modulation be therapeutically leveraged to boost 1A CAR function without inducing off‑target translation effects?
